Key Finding / Executive Summary

**Overall novelty indication: not_novel**

A prior-art search was conducted for a film edge opening system centered on a suction device with a movable section, passive return bias, and a movement-limiting element intended to reduce pressure pockets and deformation during opening. The search identified multiple earlier references that, collectively and in several cases individually, cover the core feature set at least partially [US4458891A; US6254089B1; US3673759A; US11289354B2; US5332206A].

The strongest references fall into three main groups: (i) direct film/bag opening references using suction to open a folded or tubular film/bag [US11912443B2; JP05553925B2; US3706180A; TWM621865U], (ii) movable/compliant suction head references with passive spring return [US11289354B2; DE102021203017A1; US5688008A], and (iii) movable suction head assemblies with explicit travel limiting, retention, guide, or stop structures [US4458891A; US6254089B1; US5332206A; US20090039589A1; US7926802B2; US3937457A].

### Types of Solutions Identified
- Suction-pad film or bag opening by separating opposed suction members [US11912443B2; JP05553925B2; EP4140907B1]
- Compliant or pivotable suction heads that accommodate deformation and return to a home orientation [US11289354B2; DE102021203017A1]
- Spring-biased telescoping or vertically movable suction nozzles with explicit travel restriction or upper-limit control [US4458891A; US5332206A; US20090039589A1; US7926802B2]
- Bag-opening suction mechanisms with elastic seat / shock absorption / tolerance accommodation [TWM621865U]
- Sheet/bag separation devices with limited relative movement and resilient retention in a datum condition [US3673759A]

### Gap Analysis Table

| Feature | Summary of Prior-Art Coverage | Coverage Level | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| F1 Suction Film Edge Opener | Directly covered by multiple film/bag/sheet suction-opening refs [US11912443B2; JP05553925B2; US3706180A; US3673759A] | STRONG | No meaningful gap found |
| F2 Movable Compliant Section | Covered by pivotable, swingable, telescoping, movable, and floating suction head refs [US11289354B2; US4458891A; US6254089B1; US5688008A] | STRONG | Well supported |
| F3 Passive Bias Return Element | Covered by elastic component / spring return / resilient urging refs [US11289354B2; DE102021203017A1; US4458891A; US6254089B1; US5332206A] | STRONG | Well supported |
| F4 Movement Limiting Element | Covered by explicit stop, upper-end limit, guide restriction, limited movement, or retention structures [US6254089B1; US4458891A; US5332206A; US20090039589A1; US3937457A] | STRONG | Stronger support emerged in Round 2 |
| F5 Deformation Reduction During Opening | Covered directly or partially by refs addressing deformation, wrinkling avoidance, shock absorption, or gentler suction interaction [US11289354B2; TWM621865U; CN221050062U; US11912443B2] | STRONG | Often expressed as tolerance accommodation / deformation handling |
| F6 Integrated Combination | Several references or close combinations cover suction + movable section + passive return + movement limiting [US4458891A; US6254089B1; US3673759A; US5332206A] | STRONG | Core combination is well covered in adjacent art |

### Technology Trends
- Earlier sheet-feeding and printing-machine suction heads already used spring-biased telescoping or limited-motion suction assemblies [US4458891A; US5332206A; US20090039589A1].
- Packaging and film-opening references adapt suction opening to films, bags, and tubular sleeves [US11912443B2; JP05553925B2; TWM621865U; EP4140907B1].
- More recent references emphasize compliant suction structures that reduce deformation, improve tolerance handling, and avoid rigid contact [US11289354B2; CN221050062U; TWM621865U].

### Risk Assessment
Novelty risk is **high** from a prior-art coverage standpoint because each core feature has at least one earlier independent reference with Y or Y1 coverage, and the integrated mechanical architecture is well represented across adjacent suction-handling art [US4458891A; US6254089B1; US3673759A; US11289354B2].

| Item | Details |
|---|---|
| Objective | Assess novelty by searching prior art relevant to a film edge opening system using a suction device with a movable section, passive return bias, and a movement-limiting element |
| In-Scope | Patent and non-patent prior art relevant to film handling, bag opening, sheet/web separation, suction heads, compliant mounts, spring return, and movement-limiting structures |
| Out-of-Scope | Freedom-to-operate, patentability opinion, infringement, claim drafting, filing strategy, market analysis |
| Authorities | Patent literature and surfaced literature equivalents reviewed through keyword, semantic, structural-combination, and citation-led search methods |
| Languages | Primarily English machine-rendered records; some JP/CN/DE/TW/SU family records reviewed in translated metadata |
| Known Constraints | Some non-US records were available primarily via translated abstracts/DWPI fields rather than native full text |
| Open Questions | None remaining for this novelty search; defaults were confirmed before feature locking |
| Assumptions | Generic film-edge opening context across web-handling systems; movable section may be translational or pivoting; passive biasing includes springs or equivalent passive restoring members; limiter may limit stroke and/or prevent unintended separation |
| Feature Confirmation | User confirmed the six-feature matrix before research began |

**Pin-cites / notable passages:**
- **US4458891A**: Claim 1 — “intermediate nozzle… resiliently urged”; “suction nozzle… resiliently urged”; “guide member… vertically restricting the flange”; “guide member being limited in its vertical position by said cam mechanism.”
- **US6254089B1**: Abstract / DWPI description — suction head “connected to the holding arm via a spring” and movement “restricted by stop means”; Claim 1 recites movable connection via spring and guide accommodation.
- **US5332206A**: Claim 1 — “tension coil spring… biasing said intermediate nozzle upward”; “compression coil spring… biasing said suction port member downward”; “a stopper… for regulating an upper end limit of said intermediate nozzle.”
- **US3673759A**: Claim 1 — “limited movement relative one another” and “resiliently retaining said cups and second means in a datum condition.”
- **US11289354B2 / DE102021203017A1**: Claim 1 — elastic component “permits a swing… according to tilt or deformation” and “returns… to a predetermined orientation.”

## 7. NPL Record View

No non-patent literature reference rose to A- or B-level significance in the screened set. The novelty assessment therefore rests primarily on patent references located through keyword, semantic, structural-combination, and citation-led searching.

## 8. Transactional Search Summary (Client-Facing)

### Search Approach
- Scope: Film edge opening / bag mouth opening / web separation using suction, with movable compliant section, passive return bias, and movement limitation
- Databases/Tools: Patent database search, semantic search, structural-combination search, and citation-network expansion
- Keywords used: suction pad, vacuum opener, film opener, bag opening, movable suction head, swingable joint, elastic component, spring-biased, stopper, travel limit, upper-end limit, retainer, limited movement, resiliently retaining, deformation, wrinkle reduction
- Time period: No strict cutoff; older foundational sheet/suction mechanisms were intentionally included
- Search protocol: Round 1 broad search across all features; Round 2 targeted gap-filling for explicit limiter/retention/anti-separation references

### Key Findings
- Total references screened: dozens across keyword/semantic/citation paths
- Unique relevant references retained in report: 22
- Relevance breakdown: A-level ≈ 8, B-level ≈ 14, C-level/peripheral several more
- X-category count: 3 strong integrated references in the feature matrix (US4458891A; US6254089B1; US5332206A)

### Next Actions
- Review the closest integrated references in full native-family form if needed for deeper claim-element comparison
- Extend into additional non-English families and family members if broader international landscaping is desired
- Treat the prior-art coverage as a search evidence base rather than a legal opinion

- Review the three closest integrated references first: **US4458891A**, **US6254089B1**, and **US5332206A**.
- Compare the invention’s specific implementation details against the exact mechanical teachings in those references, especially whether the claimed “movement limiting element” is structurally different from their stop/guide/collar/limit arrangements.

### Optional Extended Search
- Expand into additional JP/CN/TW families for packaging-machine bag opening mechanisms
- Search more deeply for direct film-edge opening references that explicitly combine spring return with captive-limited suction travel
- Add broader non-patent review if engineering literature on web-handling suction heads is important

### Monitoring
- Monitor future filings in bag opening, web handling, and suction-head compliant pickup systems
- Watch classifications around sheet feeding, bag opening, and vacuum pickup head structures

### Prototype Validation
- From an engineering standpoint, test whether the claimed structure produces measurable reduction in wrinkles, stretching, or pressure-pocket formation beyond the accommodation effects already shown in prior art [US11289354B2; TWM621865U; CN221050062U]

### Market Analysis
That is outside my scope. I specialize in novelty assessment through prior art search.

### Search Quality & Limitations
- Search quality: good multi-path coverage with keyword, semantic, structural-combination, and citation-based expansion
- Known limitations: some foreign-language references were reviewed mainly through machine-rendered abstract/DWPI content; some family members may contain additional detail not surfaced in the retrieved excerpts
- QA checklist: core features all reached strong evidence coverage; closest integrated references were retrieved in full-text patent-detail form before final mapping

## Novelty Assessment

Feature-by-feature synthesis indicates that the core suction-based opening context (F1) is directly disclosed in earlier film/bag opening references [US11912443B2; JP05553925B2; US3673759A]. The movable/compliant suction section (F2) and passive return bias (F3) are clearly taught in earlier spring-biased, pivotable, telescoping, or floating suction head references [US11289354B2; US4458891A; US6254089B1; US5332206A]. The movement-limiting element (F4), initially the weakest feature, is also expressly present in earlier stop-limited or upper-end-limited suction head assemblies [US6254089B1; US5332206A; US20090039589A1; US3937457A].

The functional objective of reducing deformation, wrinkling, air/bubble effects, or rough suction engagement (F5) is also reflected in earlier art, although not always using identical wording [US11289354B2; TWM621865U; CN221050062U]. Finally, the integrated combination (F6) is well represented by earlier references that combine suction engagement, compliant movement, spring return, and travel-limiting structure in a single assembly [US4458891A; US6254089B1; US5332206A], even where the application context is sheet handling rather than film-edge opening.

Under the required evidence rule for this report, every core feature has at least one independent earlier reference with Y or Y1 coverage, and several earlier references show the combined architecture at a high level of similarity.

**Verdict: not_novel**

## Risk Assessment

**Aggregate novelty risk: High**

The top anticipating references are **US4458891A**, **US6254089B1**, and **US5332206A** because they each disclose a suction head assembly with movable/compliant structure, passive spring return, and explicit movement limitation through guides, stops, or upper-end limit regulation. Additional direct film/bag-opening references such as **US3673759A**, **US11912443B2**, and **JP05553925B2** reinforce that the application context itself is also well represented.

## Limitations

- Search relied primarily on patent literature; no NPL reference rose to A/B significance
- Some foreign-language records were reviewed through machine-translated or abstract-level content plus DWPI-style summaries rather than native prosecution documents
- Exact mechanical details of every family member were not exhaustively harmonized across all jurisdictions
- The search was targeted to novelty/prior art coverage and does not address patentability, infringement, or freedom-to-operate
- Later unpublished applications or non-indexed regional utility models may not be captured

## Verdict

**Verdict: not_novel**

The prior-art record shows that suction-based opening of flexible film/bag/sheet material, movable/compliant suction sections, passive spring return, and explicit movement-limiting structures were all known before the disclosed invention date, including in integrated suction-head assemblies. ## Customer Idea
Film Edge Opening System

Technical Field: Film handling / web handling.

Problem: Unwanted pressure pockets, deformation such as wrinkling or stretching during film opening.

Key technical aspects: A suction device with a movable section for film edge opening. One or more biasing elements are configured to return the movable section to a reference position. A movement limiting element prevents excessive movement or unintended separation of the movable section.

## Clarifications
Default assumptions confirmed by user:
1. Application type: generic film-edge opening across web-handling systems.
2. Movable section motion: either translational or pivoting compliant movement.
3. Biasing element type: any passive restoring element.
4. Limiter function: both limit stroke and prevent unintended separation.
5. Novelty focus: integrated combination of suction + movable section + biasing + limiter for film edge opening.

## Confirmed Scope
A film-edge opening system in which a suction device engages and opens a film edge. The suction device includes a movable/compliant section that can displace during interaction with the film, one or more passive biasing elements that return the movable section to a reference position, and a movement-limiting structure that constrains travel and prevents unintended separation. The assessment will focus on the novelty of this integrated combination in film-handling systems, especially for reducing pressure pockets, wrinkling, and stretching during opening.

Key Finding / Executive Summary

**Overall novelty indication: not_novel**

A prior-art search was conducted for a film edge opening system centered on a suction device with a movable section, passive return bias, and a movement-limiting element intended to reduce pressure pockets and deformation during opening. The search identified multiple earlier references that, collectively and in several cases individually, cover the core feature set at least partially [US4458891A; US6254089B1; US3673759A; US11289354B2; US5332206A].

The strongest references fall into three main groups: (i) direct film/bag opening references using suction to open a folded or tubular film/bag [US11912443B2; JP05553925B2; US3706180A; TWM621865U], (ii) movable/compliant suction head references with passive spring return [US11289354B2; DE102021203017A1; US5688008A], and (iii) movable suction head assemblies with explicit travel limiting, retention, guide, or stop structures [US4458891A; US6254089B1; US5332206A; US20090039589A1; US7926802B2; US3937457A].

### Types of Solutions Identified
- Suction-pad film or bag opening by separating opposed suction members [US11912443B2; JP05553925B2; EP4140907B1]
- Compliant or pivotable suction heads that accommodate deformation and return to a home orientation [US11289354B2; DE102021203017A1]
- Spring-biased telescoping or vertically movable suction nozzles with explicit travel restriction or upper-limit control [US4458891A; US5332206A; US20090039589A1; US7926802B2]
- Bag-opening suction mechanisms with elastic seat / shock absorption / tolerance accommodation [TWM621865U]
- Sheet/bag separation devices with limited relative movement and resilient retention in a datum condition [US3673759A]

### Gap Analysis Table

| Feature | Summary of Prior-Art Coverage | Coverage Level | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| F1 Suction Film Edge Opener | Directly covered by multiple film/bag/sheet suction-opening refs [US11912443B2; JP05553925B2; US3706180A; US3673759A] | STRONG | No meaningful gap found |
| F2 Movable Compliant Section | Covered by pivotable, swingable, telescoping, movable, and floating suction head refs [US11289354B2; US4458891A; US6254089B1; US5688008A] | STRONG | Well supported |
| F3 Passive Bias Return Element | Covered by elastic component / spring return / resilient urging refs [US11289354B2; DE102021203017A1; US4458891A; US6254089B1; US5332206A] | STRONG | Well supported |
| F4 Movement Limiting Element | Covered by explicit stop, upper-end limit, guide restriction, limited movement, or retention structures [US6254089B1; US4458891A; US5332206A; US20090039589A1; US3937457A] | STRONG | Stronger support emerged in Round 2 |
| F5 Deformation Reduction During Opening | Covered directly or partially by refs addressing deformation, wrinkling avoidance, shock absorption, or gentler suction interaction [US11289354B2; TWM621865U; CN221050062U; US11912443B2] | STRONG | Often expressed as tolerance accommodation / deformation handling |
| F6 Integrated Combination | Several references or close combinations cover suction + movable section + passive return + movement limiting [US4458891A; US6254089B1; US3673759A; US5332206A] | STRONG | Core combination is well covered in adjacent art |

### Technology Trends
- Earlier sheet-feeding and printing-machine suction heads already used spring-biased telescoping or limited-motion suction assemblies [US4458891A; US5332206A; US20090039589A1].
- Packaging and film-opening references adapt suction opening to films, bags, and tubular sleeves [US11912443B2; JP05553925B2; TWM621865U; EP4140907B1].
- More recent references emphasize compliant suction structures that reduce deformation, improve tolerance handling, and avoid rigid contact [US11289354B2; CN221050062U; TWM621865U].

### Risk Assessment
Novelty risk is **high** from a prior-art coverage standpoint because each core feature has at least one earlier independent reference with Y or Y1 coverage, and the integrated mechanical architecture is well represented across adjacent suction-handling art [US4458891A; US6254089B1; US3673759A; US11289354B2].

| Assumptions | Generic film-edge opening context across web-handling systems; movable section may be translational or pivoting; passive biasing includes springs or equivalent passive restoring members; limiter may limit stroke and/or prevent unintended separation |

## Novelty Assessment

Feature-by-feature synthesis indicates that the core suction-based opening context (F1) is directly disclosed in earlier film/bag opening references [US11912443B2; JP05553925B2; US3673759A]. The movable/compliant suction section (F2) and passive return bias (F3) are clearly taught in earlier spring-biased, pivotable, telescoping, or floating suction head references [US11289354B2; US4458891A; US6254089B1; US5332206A]. The movement-limiting element (F4), initially the weakest feature, is also expressly present in earlier stop-limited or upper-end-limited suction head assemblies [US6254089B1; US5332206A; US20090039589A1; US3937457A].

The functional objective of reducing deformation, wrinkling, air/bubble effects, or rough suction engagement (F5) is also reflected in earlier art, although not always using identical wording [US11289354B2; TWM621865U; CN221050062U]. Finally, the integrated combination (F6) is well represented by earlier references that combine suction engagement, compliant movement, spring return, and travel-limiting structure in a single assembly [US4458891A; US6254089B1; US5332206A], even where the application context is sheet handling rather than film-edge opening.

Under the required evidence rule for this report, every core feature has at least one independent earlier reference with Y or Y1 coverage, and several earlier references show the combined architecture at a high level of similarity.

**Verdict: not_novel**

## Risk Assessment

**Aggregate novelty risk: High**

The top anticipating references are **US4458891A**, **US6254089B1**, and **US5332206A** because they each disclose a suction head assembly with movable/compliant structure, passive spring return, and explicit movement limitation through guides, stops, or upper-end limit regulation. Additional direct film/bag-opening references such as **US3673759A**, **US11912443B2**, and **JP05553925B2** reinforce that the application context itself is also well represented.

## Limitations

- Search relied primarily on patent literature; no NPL reference rose to A/B significance
- Some foreign-language records were reviewed through machine-translated or abstract-level content plus DWPI-style summaries rather than native prosecution documents
- Exact mechanical details of every family member were not exhaustively harmonized across all jurisdictions
- The search was targeted to novelty/prior art coverage and does not address patentability, infringement, or freedom-to-operate
- Later unpublished applications or non-indexed regional utility models may not be captured

## Verdict

**Verdict: not_novel**

The prior-art record shows that suction-based opening of flexible film/bag/sheet material, movable/compliant suction sections, passive spring return, and explicit movement-limiting structures were all known before the disclosed invention date, including in integrated suction-head assemblies. Although the exact application-specific packaging implementation may differ in detail, the core features as defined for this novelty search are covered by earlier independent references, especially **Invention Title**: Height Adjust Actuation Mechanism  
**Domain**: Mechanical height-adjustment hardware for tables or similar furniture columns  
**Key Technical Problem**: Improve the height-adjustment process by providing a simpler manually actuated lock/release mechanism for a telescoping support tube, while allowing assisted movement when released  
**Proposed Solution**: A threaded screw shaft engages a boss on an outer tube and, in an engaged position, applies frictional force to the outer surface of an inner telescoping tube. Rotating the screw to a disengaged position relieves the friction force so the inner tube can move, assisted by a gas spring. A friction pad may be attached to or engaged by the screw end, and an intermediate friction element may be placed between the screw end and inner tube to distribute pressure more uniformly.  
**Key Claims**:  
- Screw-shaft-based manual locking and releasing of a telescoping inner tube  
- Frictional clamping of the inner tube by the screw end  
- Release of clamping force by screw rotation to permit height adjustment  
- Optional friction pad at the screw end  
- Optional intermediate friction element for pressure distribution  
- Use with gas-spring-assisted movement

## Open Questions with Proposed Defaults

For each question below, **"Default if confirmed"** shows what will be assumed if you reply "Confirm defaults".

1. **What is the intended product context for the mechanism?**  
   Options: (a) height-adjustable table leg/column, (b) chair/post column, (c) general telescoping support column, (d) broader linear support mechanism  
   → **Default if confirmed: (a) height-adjustable table leg/column** — this is expressly stated in the technical field

2. **How should the core locking action be interpreted?**  
   Options: (a) direct radial friction locking of inner tube by a threaded screw, (b) positive detent/latch mechanism, (c) cam lock, (d) collet-style clamp  
   → **Default if confirmed: (a) direct radial friction locking of inner tube by a threaded screw** — this is the clearest reading of the disclosure

3. **How should the gas spring be treated in the novelty search scope?**  
   Options: (a) supporting feature that assists motion after release, (b) essential core feature, (c) optional embodiment only, (d) out of scope  
   → **Default if confirmed: (a) supporting feature that assists motion after release** — the main mechanism appears to be the screw-based friction lock/release system

4. **What should be treated as the most specific implementation of the contact interface?**  
   Options: (a) screw end contacts inner tube directly, (b) screw end acts through a friction pad, (c) screw end acts through an intermediate pressure-distribution element, (d) both (b) and (c) as optional variants around the same core concept  
   → **Default if confirmed: (d) both (b) and (c) as optional variants around the same core concept** — the text presents these as alternative or additional embodiments

5. **What level of scope should be used for the novelty search?**  
   Options: (a) mechanism broadly covering telescoping tube friction-lock systems, (b) table-specific implementations only, (c) gas-spring-assisted table columns only, (d) only the exact disclosed embodiment  
   → **Default if confirmed: (a) mechanism broadly covering telescoping tube friction-lock systems** — broader prior art scoping is more appropriate for novelty assessment

## Defaults Summary

|---|----------|----------------|
| 1 | Intended product context | Height-adjustable table leg/column |
| 2 | Core locking action | Direct radial friction locking by threaded screw |
| 3 | Role of gas spring | Supporting feature that assists motion after release |
| 4 | Contact interface scope | Friction pad and intermediate element as optional variants |
| 5 | Search scope level | Broad telescoping tube friction-lock systems |

Height adjustable actuation mechanism for a table column using a threaded screw shaft engaged with a boss on an outer tube. Optional friction pad and optional intermediate friction element may be provided between the screw end and inner tube.

## Clarifications

- Core locking action: direct radial friction locking by threaded screw
- Gas spring: supporting feature assisting motion after release

## Confirmed Scope
Novelty assessment will focus on mechanical telescoping support columns for height-adjustable tables, particularly screw-actuated friction locking and release mechanisms acting between outer and inner tubes, with optional friction pad/intermediate pressure element and optional gas spring assistance.

Key Finding / Executive Summary

**Overall novelty indication: not_novel**

A prior-art search of telescoping support-column and adjustable post mechanisms identified multiple earlier references teaching the core combination of: (i) an outer tube and inner telescoping tube, (ii) a threaded screw or threaded fastener carried by one tube, (iii) friction-based locking of the telescoping relation, and (iv) release by screw loosening or reverse rotation [USRE39661E1; US8297870B2; US7726898B2; US9388837B1; US8851435B1]. The closest reference identified in this search is **USRE39661E1**, which expressly discloses a fixed tube and telescope tube, with threaded holes/nuts on one tube and a set screw bearing against the other tube to selectively lock the telescoping position [USRE39661E1, claim 8; DWPI Novelty].

The optional features are also represented in the surrounding prior art landscape: pressure-distribution/clamping intermediary structures appear in telescoping locks such as drum-set or pole clamping mechanisms [US6977332B2; US8297870B2], and gas-spring-assisted telescoping rod/column arrangements appear in references such as EP1155244A1 [EP1155244A1, claim 1].

### Types of Solutions Identified

1. **Direct set-screw-on-tube locks** — outer tube carries one or more threaded holes/nuts and a set screw bears directly on the inner telescope tube to lock at a selected position [USRE39661E1].
2. **Thread-driven internal friction expanders** — screw motion expands an internal sleeve/spreader to frictionally lock telescoping tubes [US9388837B1; US8851435B1; US8297870B2; US7726898B2].
3. **External clamping hub/collar systems** — an outer collar, fixing hub, or movable arm clamps the inner tube by a tightening member [US6977332B2].
4. **Gas-spring telescoping rod systems** — telescopic rods/columns including gas spring assistance plus a locking device [EP1155244A1].

### Gap Analysis Table

| Feature | Description | Coverage Summary | Gap Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| F1 | Telescoping outer/inner tube column | Repeatedly disclosed in many references [USRE39661E1; US6977332B2; US9388837B1; US8297870B2] | No meaningful gap |
| F2 | Threaded screw engaged with boss/threaded support | Expressly disclosed in set-screw / threaded-nut or threaded-screw arrangements [USRE39661E1; US5842671A; US11313510B2] | No meaningful gap |
| F3 | Screw-end or screw-actuated friction locking | Expressly or partially disclosed across direct screw-bearing and threaded friction-lock references [USRE39661E1; US9388837B1; US8851435B1; US8297870B2] | No meaningful gap |
| F4 | Rotational release to permit adjustment | Expressly disclosed by loosening / reverse rotation / unlocking rotation [US9388837B1; US8851435B1; US5842671A; WO1998020214A1] | No meaningful gap |
| F5 | Friction pad / intermediate pressure element | Partial-to-moderate coverage in hub/collar/spreader or intermediary-force structures [US6977332B2; US8297870B2] | Narrow embodiment detail only |
| F6 | Gas spring assisted movement | Disclosed in telescoping rod/column context [EP1155244A1; semantically supported in assisted column art] | Combination detail only |

### Technology / Prior-Art Trends

- The search landscape is dense around **telescoping tube locking**.
- The same core mechanical idea appears across **support posts, rods, poles, stands, platforms, and furniture-adjacent mechanisms**, not just tables [USRE39661E1; US6977332B2; US8297870B2].
- Prior art frequently solves the same problem using either **direct screw pressure**, **collet/spreader friction locking**, or **clamping hubs/collars**.
- Gas-spring assistance appears as an adjacent but established subtheme in telescoping support/rod systems [EP1155244A1].

### Risk Assessment

**Aggregate novelty risk: High**

This is a prior-art coverage summary, not a legal patentability opinion. The core mechanism is closely represented in earlier references, especially direct threaded fastener locking of one telescoping tube relative to another [USRE39661E1], reinforced by multiple screw-actuated friction-locking telescopic assemblies [US9388837B1; US8297870B2].

## Novelty Assessment

Feature-by-feature synthesis based on the references identified above:

- **F1 (telescoping outer/inner tube column)** is plainly established across multiple earlier references [USRE39661E1; US6977332B2; US9388837B1; US8297870B2].
- **F2 (threaded screw engaged with boss/threaded support on outer member)** is expressly shown in direct form in USRE39661E1, which recites a tube having a threaded hole and set screw bearing against the other tube [USRE39661E1, claim 8], and is also supported more broadly by threaded engagement mechanisms in telescoping columns/posts [US5842671A; US11313510B2].
- **F3 (screw-end or screw-actuated friction locking)** is disclosed either directly by set screw bearing on the other tube [USRE39661E1] or functionally by screw-driven friction lock/spreader systems [US9388837B1; US8297870B2; US7726898B2].
- **F4 (release by loosening / reverse rotation)** is shown or inherently taught in multiple references where reverse rotation or loosening releases the clamping or locking condition [US9388837B1; US8851435B1; US5842671A; WO1998020214A1].
- **F5 (friction pad / intermediate pressure element)** appears as a narrower implementation detail; intermediary clamping/contact structures are visible in references such as US6977332B2 and the spreader-based LEKI family [US6977332B2; US8297870B2].
- **F6 (gas spring assist)** is found in telescoping rod context in EP1155244A1 [EP1155244A1, claim 1].

Taken together, the search results show that every core feature has at least one independent earlier reference with Y or Y1 coverage, and the broad core combination is strongly represented by prior art across adjacent telescoping support mechanisms [USRE39661E1; US9388837B1; US8297870B2].

**Verdict: not_novel**

## Risk Assessment

**High** prior-art coverage risk. The top anticipating references are **USRE39661E1**, **US9388837B1**, and **US8297870B2**. USRE39661E1 is the closest direct structural analog because it expressly teaches a threaded hole/set screw arrangement on one telescoping tube bearing against the other tube to lock position, while US9388837B1 and US8297870B2 reinforce the broader screw-actuated friction-lock and rotational-release concepts in telescoping tube assemblies.

## Limitations

- Search emphasized patent literature over non-patent literature because the invention is a mechanical tube-locking arrangement.
- English-language accessible patent records were prioritized; some foreign-language utility-model coverage may remain unreviewed.
- No exhaustive legal-status review was performed.
- Optional features F5/F6 were covered to a moderate, not exhaustive, level.
- Some semantic-search outputs were used for concept expansion but not retained as citation-verified final references.

## Verdict

**Verdict: not_novel**

The core mechanism disclosed in the invention is closely matched by earlier telescoping-tube locking references, particularly USRE39661E1 for the direct set-screw/threaded-hole arrangement and several additional references for screw-driven friction locking and release. On the evidence identified in this search, the broad inventive concept is already represented in the prior art, with only narrower embodiment details potentially remaining as differentiators.', ## Customer Idea
Height adjustable actuation mechanism for a table column using a threaded screw shaft engaged with a boss on an outer tube. In an engaged position, the screw applies friction force to an inner support tube to lock height. In a disengaged position, the friction force is relieved and the tube can move, assisted by a gas spring. Optional friction pad and optional intermediate friction element may be provided between the screw end and inner tube.

## Clarifications
Defaults confirmed by user.
- Product context: height-adjustable table leg/column
- Core locking action: direct radial friction locking by threaded screw
- Gas spring: supporting feature assisting motion after release
- Contact interface: friction pad and intermediate element treated as optional variants
- Search scope: broad telescoping tube friction-lock systems

## Confirmed Scope
Novelty assessment will focus on mechanical telescoping support columns for height-adjustable tables, particularly screw-actuated friction locking and release mechanisms acting between outer and inner tubes, with optional friction pad/intermediate pressure element and optional gas spring assistance.

## Patent Search Results
Keyword-precision path identified strong analogous telescoping support references, including USRE39661E1 (receiver tube + telescope tube locked by set screws threaded into nuts on the outer tube), US6977332B2 (telescopic drum support with fastening element/hub clamping the inner tube), US9388837B1 and US8851435B1 (twist-lock telescoping tube assemblies using threaded components and friction locking), plus several B-level telescoping support references.

## Semantic Search Results
Semantic search confirmed a dense concept family around telescoping supports locked by threaded members pressing on inner sliding members, including alternative vocabulary such as set screw, thumb screw, compression screw, threaded boss, threaded insert, pressure shoe, anti-marring pad, and spring-assisted column. It indicated moderate semantic support for optional friction pad/intermediate element and gas-spring assistance.

## Combination Search Results
Combination-focused search found LEKI adjustable pole references US8297870B2 and US7726898B2 covering telescoping tubes, threaded actuation, friction locking, and release for adjustment, though with indirect spreader mechanisms. EP1155244A1 surfaced as a relevant telescopic rod with gas spring assist.

- pressure shoe / swivel tip / anti-marring pad: variants of intermediate friction/contact element
- spring-assisted column / gas spring assisted column: assisted-motion vocabulary for F6

- US8297870B2 — strong multi-feature telescoping friction-lock reference
- EP1155244A1 — useful for gas spring assisted telescopic rod concept

'/final_report.md', 'content': '## 1. Key Finding / Executive Summary

**Overall novelty indication: not_novel**

A prior-art search of telescoping support-column and adjustable post mechanisms identified multiple earlier references teaching the core combination of: (i) an outer tube and inner telescoping tube, (ii) a threaded screw or threaded fastener carried by one tube, (iii) friction-based locking of the telescoping relation, and (iv) release by screw loosening or reverse rotation [USRE39661E1; US8297870B2; US7726898B2; US9388837B1; US8851435B1]. The closest reference identified in this search is **USRE39661E1**, which expressly discloses a fixed tube and telescope tube, with threaded holes/nuts on one tube and a set screw bearing against the other tube to selectively lock the telescoping position [USRE39661E1, claim 8; DWPI Novelty].

The optional features are also represented in the surrounding prior art landscape: pressure-distribution/clamping intermediary structures appear in telescoping locks such as drum-set or pole clamping mechanisms [US6977332B2; US8297870B2], and gas-spring-assisted telescoping rod/column arrangements appear in references such as EP1155244A1 [EP1155244A1, claim 1].

### Types of Solutions Identified

1. **Direct set-screw-on-tube locks** — outer tube carries one or more threaded holes/nuts and a set screw bears directly on the inner telescope tube [USRE39661E1].
2. **Thread-driven internal friction expanders** — screw motion expands an internal sleeve/spreader to frictionally lock telescoping tubes [US9388837B1; US8851435B1; US8297870B2; US7726898B2].
3. **External clamping hub/collar systems** — an outer collar, fixing hub, or movable arm clamps the inner tube by a tightening member [US6977332B2].
4. **Gas-spring telescoping rod systems** — telescopic rods/columns including gas spring assistance plus a locking device [EP1155244A1].

### Gap Analysis Table

| Feature | Description | Coverage Summary | Gap Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| F1 | Telescoping outer/inner tube column | Repeatedly disclosed in many references [USRE39661E1; US6977332B2; US9388837B1; US8297870B2] | No meaningful gap |
| F2 | Threaded screw engaged with boss/threaded support | Expressly disclosed in set-screw / threaded-nut or threaded-screw arrangements [USRE39661E1; US5842671A; US11313510B2] | No meaningful gap |
| F3 | Screw-end or screw-actuated friction locking | Expressly or partially disclosed across direct screw-bearing and threaded friction-lock references [USRE39661E1; US9388837B1; US8851435B1; US8297870B2] | No meaningful gap |
| F4 | Rotational release to permit adjustment | Expressly disclosed by loosening / reverse rotation / unlocking rotation [US9388837B1; US8851435B1; US5842671A; WO1998020214A1] | No meaningful gap |
| F5 | Friction pad / intermediate pressure element | Partial-to-moderate coverage in hub/collar/spreader or intermediary-force structures [US6977332B2; US8297870B2] | Narrow embodiment detail only |
| F6 | Gas spring assisted movement | Disclosed in telescoping rod/column context [EP1155244A1; semantically supported in assisted column art] | Combination detail only |

### Technology / Prior-Art Trends

- The search landscape is dense around **telescoping tube locking**.
- The same core mechanical idea appears across **support posts, rods, poles, stands, platforms, and furniture-adjacent mechanisms**, not just tables [USRE39661E1; US6977332B2; US8297870B2].
- Prior art frequently solves the same problem using either **direct screw pressure**, **collet/spreader friction locking**, or **clamping hubs/collars**.
- Gas-spring assistance appears as an adjacent but established subtheme in telescoping support/rod systems [EP1155244A1].

### Risk Assessment

**Aggregate novelty risk: High**

This is a prior-art coverage summary, not a legal patentability opinion. The core mechanism is closely represented in earlier references, especially direct threaded fastener locking of one telescoping tube relative to another [USRE39661E1], reinforced by multiple screw-actuated friction-locking telescopic assemblies [US9388837B1; US8297870B2].

## Novelty Assessment

Feature-by-feature synthesis based on the references identified above:

- **F1 (telescoping outer/inner tube column)** is plainly established across multiple earlier references [USRE39661E1; US6977332B2; US9388837B1; US8297870B2].
- **F2 (threaded screw engaged with boss/threaded support on outer member)** is expressly shown in direct form in USRE39661E1, which recites a tube having a threaded hole and set screw bearing against the other tube [USRE39661E1, claim 8], and is also supported more broadly by threaded engagement mechanisms in telescoping columns/posts [US5842671A; US11313510B2].
- **F3 (screw-end or screw-actuated friction locking)** is disclosed either directly by set screw bearing on the other tube [USRE39661E1] or functionally by screw-driven friction lock/spreader systems [US9388837B1; US8297870B2; US7726898B2].
- **F4 (release by loosening / reverse rotation)** is shown or inherently taught in multiple references where reverse rotation or loosening releases the clamping or locking condition [US9388837B1; US8851435B1; US5842671A; WO1998020214A1].
- **F5 (friction pad / intermediate pressure element)** appears as a narrower implementation detail; intermediary clamping/contact structures are visible in references such as US6977332B2 and the spreader-based LEKI family [US6977332B2; US8297870B2].
- **F6 (gas spring assist)** is found in telescoping rod context in EP1155244A1 [EP1155244A1, claim 1].

Taken together, the search results show that every core feature has at least one independent earlier reference with Y or Y1 coverage, and the broad core combination is strongly represented by prior art across adjacent telescoping support mechanisms [USRE39661E1; US9388837B1; US8297870B2].

**Verdict: not_novel**

## Risk Assessment

**High** prior-art coverage risk. The top anticipating references are **USRE39661E1**, **US9388837B1**, and **US8297870B2**. USRE39661E1 is the closest direct structural analog because it expressly teaches a threaded hole/set screw arrangement on one telescoping tube bearing against the other tube to lock position, while US9388837B1 and US8297870B2 reinforce the broader screw-actuated friction-lock and rotational-release concepts in telescoping tube assemblies.

## Limitations

- Search emphasized patent literature over non-patent literature because the invention is a mechanical tube-locking arrangement.
- English-language accessible patent records were prioritized; some foreign-language utility-model coverage may remain unreviewed.
- No exhaustive legal-status review was performed.
- Optional features F5/F6 were covered to a moderate, not exhaustive, level.
- Some semantic-search outputs were used for concept expansion but not retained as citation-verified final references.

## Verdict

**Verdict: not_novel**

The core mechanism disclosed in the invention is closely matched by earlier telescoping-tube locking references, particularly USRE39661E1 for the direct set-screw/threaded-hole arrangement and several additional references for screw-driven friction locking and release.
